item.title: Watch Live: FBI gives update on fireworks explosion outside NYC federal building
item.description: NYPD sources said fireworks were found in a garbage can and a man was taken into custody outside the federal building in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: Judge temporarily halts Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
item.description: The ruling comes after a coalition of 12 states sued to block the deal, arguing it would harm consumers and the entertainment industry.

item.title: Darline Graham, Lindsey Graham's sister, says she'll run for full Senate term
item.description: Sen. Darline Graham, who is serving out the remainder of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham's term in the Senate, said she will seek a full term for the South Carolina seat her brother held for more than two decades.

item.title: Former FBI agent on explosion outside New York City federal building
item.description: Former FBI Special Agent Tim Gallagher breaks down what New York City police will be looking for in the wake of Monday's explosion outside a federal building in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: U.S. vows to "swiftly punish" Iran as at least 3 troops killed
item.description: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.

item.title: Early details on suspect in explosion outside NYC federal building
item.description: A suspect is in custody after an explosion outside an federal building in New York City on Monday morning, police say. Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: Tate brothers detained as they face new charges, possible U.K. extradition
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the U.K., where they face rape and sex trafficking charges.

item.title: DOJ denies policy shift after FBI agents told to stand down on ICE assault probes
item.description: HSI is an arm of ICE, meaning the immigration enforcement agency would effectively be investigating incidents involving its own agents.

item.title: Temporary restraining order granted to block Paramount Skydance-Warner Bros. merger
item.description: A judge has granted a temporary restraining order to temporarily block the Paramount Skydance-Warner Bros. Discovery merger from happening. CBS News' Jake Rosen reports. Paramount Skydance is the parent company of CBS News.

item.title: Trump to keep using old Air Force One while new plane gets security upgrades
item.description: The new Air Force One "has a lot of capability, but as I understand it, in about a month or so, they're going to send it to have it be maxed out," President Trump said Sunday.

item.title: Infamous cartel boss "El Mayo" Zambada sentenced to life in U.S. prison
item.description: Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada ordered torture and plotted murders as he Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán grew the Sinaloa cartel into the world's largest drug trafficking organization, prosecutors said.

item.title: Victims of Iranian-sponsored terror urge Trump to demand restitution in possible Iran deal
item.description: Victims of Iranian-sponsored terror shared their stories publicly on Monday and urged President Trump to demand restitution as part of a peace deal with Iran. CBS News' Jonah Kaplan has more.

item.title: Pentagon ID's 2 soldiers killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: How 401(k) fees can cost tens of thousands in retirement savings
item.description: About four in 10 workers aren't even aware they pay fees on their 401(k)s, research has found. Here's what to know.

item.title: How closely linked are gas prices to U.S.-Iran tensions?
item.description: Gas prices crept back up amid increased tensions between the U.S. and Iran. Joe Ciolli, the executive editor of Business Insider, joins with more.

item.title: Video of Moroccan man dying while pinned down by police sparks anger in Italy
item.description: The death of a Moroccan man as he was detained by Italian police in Bologna has sparked anger and a formal investigation.

item.title: Suspect arrested in explosion outside NYC federal building was anti-ICE, sources say
item.description: The suspect who was arrested after an explosion outside of 26 Federal Plaza in Manhattan was likely motivated by anti-ICE sentiments, sources say. CBS News' Anna Schecter and former NYPD Sgt. Felipe Rodriguez have more details.

item.title: Spain fans rejoice in World Cup win, but some say Trump photo-bombed team
item.description: As Spain's World Cup champs took to the stage with their trophy, President Trump joined them in their moment of glory.

item.title: Andy Burnham takes the reins as U.K.'s 7th prime minister in a decade
item.description: Having been asked by King Charles III to form a new government, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has become Britain's 7th prime minister in a decade.

item.title: Andy Burnham becomes the U.K.'s seventh new prime minister in a decade
item.description: Andy Burnham on Monday became the U.K.'s prime minister after officially being declared leader of the Labour Party last week. He's replacing its previous leader Keir Starmer. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: How much interest will a $45,000 long-term CD account earn if opened this July?
item.description: Thinking about locking today's rates on a long-term CD? A $45,000 deposit could pay off in a big way right now.

item.title: Christopher Nolan describes challenges of creating "The Odyssey"
item.description: "The Odyssey," which was shot in six countries, pulled in more than $260 million worldwide over the weekend.

item.title: Andy Burnham touts plans after becoming 7th prime minister in a decade
item.description: Andy Burnham addressed crowds outside 10 Downing Street on Monday, where he touted his plans for the U.K. after becoming Britain's seventh prime minister since 2016. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab has more.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump's claims about Iran's military capabilities
item.description: President Trump has repeatedly claimed Iran's military capabilities have been destroyed. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean joins to talk about the president's claims.

item.title: F-16s intercept planes in restricted airspace over New Jersey
item.description: F-16 fighter jets intercepted multiple planes that flew through restricted airspace over Bedminster and East Rutherford, New Jersey, on Sunday.

item.title: The U.S. labor market may soon face a new crisis: Too few workers
item.description: A severe labor shortage is set to emerge over the next decade amid a wave of baby boomer retirements and a shrinking younger workforce.

item.title: Victims of Iranian-sponsored terror want restitution as part of peace deal
item.description: A coalition of victims impacted by terrorism is demanding Iran pay restitution before any funds are returned or unfrozen as part of a peace deal.

item.title: Fire captain, wife, 3 sons found dead after flash flood in Utah
item.description: The victims were a captain in the Provo, Utah, fire department, his wife and their sons, according to fire officials.

item.title: Gas prices top $4 a gallon again as U.S.-Iran conflict escalates
item.description: The national average for a gallon of gas reached $4.003 on Monday, the first time in over a month it has crossed that threshold, AAA data shows.

item.title: What's wrong with Congress?
item.description: Congress was granted many powers under Article I of the Constitution. Nonetheless, lawmakers have ceded much of their authority to the president – and Washington has been plagued by gridlock.
